long_description:
When we create IPSec VPN on the provider gateway as an org user, BGP beighbor, IP prefix list and route maps are assigned with provider only permission group.

When we create IPSec VPN on the provider gateway as a system admin, BGP beighbor, IP prefix list and route maps are assigned with manage permission group

This looks like a bug. The customers are unable to update or delete IPSec VPN on the provider gateway due BGP permission issue
Incident source: https://github.ibm.com/tornado/ipops-vcd-cases/issues/1910 - Nishanth Kasturi (IaaS CORE)
short_description: BGP Permissions group issue when IPSec VPN on Provider gateway
comment_list:
[{"comment": "[code]GitHub: Nishanth-Kasturi has created a comment in [Broadcom 36704132] BGP Permissions group issue when IPSec VPN on Provider gateway

Shared the required details with Broadcom and had a remote session to through the issue.
Broadcom will replicate the issue in their lab and engage the engineering team [/code]", "added_by": "api.user", "added_on": "2025-12-03T15:56:15Z"}, {"comment": "[code]GitHub: Nishanth-Kasturi has edited the issue [Broadcom 36704132] BGP Permissions group issue when IPSec VPN on Provider gateway ", "added_by": "api.user", "added_on": "2025-12-03T15:55:27Z"}, {"comment": "[code]GitHub: Nishanth-Kasturi has edited the issue [Broadcom 36704132] BGP Permissions group issue when IPSec VPN on Provider gateway ", "added_by": "api.user", "added_on": "2025-12-03T15:55:05Z"}]
